reason: Both answers converge on the same core recommendation (contractor to stabilize/migrate, then move to managed K8s, avoid re-hiring a single point of failure) with similar correctness and actionable clarity. The key differentiator is that Answer A explicitly raises platform/service simplification ("consider consolidating those 40 services... likely more granularity than a team your size needs"), one of the four key points expected, while Answer B never mentions consolidating services at all — its closest analog is a caution against replatforming to a different orchestrator, which is a distinct point about migration scope, not long-term service consolidation. B adds some extra specifics (salary figures, AKS, managed DB add-ons) but these are minor value-adds rather than compensating for the missing key point. A therefore covers the full set of key points more completely.
